verdict_reason: The single disagreement entry on prahasann iva names the doctrinally substantive axis precisely: both translators drop the 'iva' (as if) for English readability, but the particle is doctrinally significant — 'it qualifies the smile, distancing it from amusement. Krishna is not smiling AT Arjuna; he is smiling in the way a settled teacher smiles when a long-deferred conversation is finally ready to begin.' This identifies the philological feature (the iva particle), the translator pattern (dropping it for readability), and the doctrinal stake (the smile is not amused; it is settled-teacher-readiness, which the commentarial tradition reads carefully). One tight line of gold-standard explanation quality. Score 9, PASS.
